active_conjecture.summary: For lambda = mu = (n-2,2), one fixed transposition/3-cycle orbit Hamiltonian appears to have raw gap 2(n-2) on the multiplicity-two target (n-3,2,1). With exactly n(n-1)(n-2) LCU terms, the normalized gap is inverse quadratic.

execution_model: Research generation and experiments run interactively during active Codex sessions. A GitHub workflow validates pushed snapshots, and a scheduled backup can commit only validation-passing changes; neither mechanism performs autonomous quantum-algorithm research between sessions.

milestones.detail: Candidates now require reductions, complexity accounting, falsifiers, classical baselines, and proof obligations before acceptance.
milestones.title: Toy circuit search was removed

milestones.detail: Exact all-target and witness-path experiments showed why fixed-depth lattice lists look good at small sizes and fail in the tail.
milestones.title: The density-one decoder optimism was reduced

milestones.detail: Successive invariants and exact reductions eliminated every generated row as a hard quantum target.
milestones.title: The current code frontier was classically exhausted

milestones.detail: Diagonal Jucys-Murphy labels are accessible, but exact multiplicity degeneracy proves that labels alone are not the internal Kronecker transform.
milestones.title: A nonabelian transform was split into precise subproblems

milestones.detail: A polynomial-description commutant Hamiltonian splits all audited blocks, including multiplicity five, after charging its LCU normalization.
milestones.title: Finite multiplicity blocks were split

next_actions.detail: Compute the exact 2x2 multiplicity action using the 2-subset permutation module, partition algebra, or orbit-character traces.
next_actions.title: Prove or kill the commutant gap formula

next_actions.detail: A one-tree multiplicity basis is insufficient because the three-copy overlapping class sums provably do not commute.
next_actions.title: Construct overlapping Racah operations

next_actions.detail: No representation label or finite spectrum matters unless it recovers a hidden involution and beats legal graph/code baselines.
next_actions.title: Demand an end-to-end natural-problem decoder

overview: The project is now useful primarily as a research filter. It has replaced tiny-circuit search with proof obligations, access-model accounting, classical attacks, exact finite representation theory, and a permanent negative-result memory. The strongest surviving lead is not an algorithm: it is a specific spectral-gap theorem that could unlock one missing transform in a nonabelian HSP route.

tracks.evidence: The all-target census evaluated millions of legal targets; the remaining question is a source-conditioned reduced-basis theorem, not another target sweep.
tracks.next: Prove a random-label LLL geometry law or abandon nearest-plane branching.
tracks.summary: Exact source models, sieve accounting, lattice attacks, and marker-aware decoders were implemented. Fixed-depth nearest-plane lists eventually collapse, so small-n decoder success is not evidence of a polynomial algorithm.
tracks.title: Hidden shift / DHSP

tracks.evidence: Current triage retains 0 viable code rows; the last Goppa syzygy collision was resolved by the hull-projector route.
tracks.next: Generate a new natural family that survives the complete classical triage gate.
tracks.summary: Goppa, quasi-cyclic, finite-geometry, rank-metric, and CFI-derived rows were attacked with support, hull, Schur, syzygy, projector, recovery, and graph-side invariants.
tracks.title: Code equivalence

tracks.evidence: YJM spectra were verified on 3 sectors, and bounded-support commutants split 2 finite multiplicity sectors up to multiplicity 5.
tracks.next: Prove the uniform normalized-gap formula, then attack coherent Racah transforms.
tracks.summary: The project separated efficient Fourier/tableau labels from the genuinely open multiplicity-space, Racah, transition-filter, and hidden-involution decoder operations.
tracks.title: Nonabelian coset states

verdict.detail: Every speedup claim remains blocked. The project has isolated one concrete representation-theoretic gap conjecture, while current hidden-shift and code-equivalence families have failed their classical tests.
verdict.title: No breakthrough yet
